root_text.an9.43:1.2: Kittāvatā nu kho, āvuso, kāyasakkhī vutto bhagavatā”ti?
root_text.an9.43:2.1: Idhāvuso, bhikkhu vivicceva kāmehi …pe… paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an9.43:2.2: Yathā yathā ca tadāyatanaṁ tathā tathā naṁ kāyena phusitvā viharati.
root_text.an9.43:2.3: Ettāvatāpi kho, āvuso, kāyasakkhī vutto bhagavatā pariyāyena.
root_text.an9.43:3.1: Puna caparaṁ, āvuso, bhikkhu vitakkavicārānaṁ vūpasamā …pe… dutiyaṁ jhānaṁ …
root_text.an9.43:3.4: Yathā yathā ca tadāyatanaṁ tathā tathā naṁ kāyena phusitvā viharati.
root_text.an9.43:3.5: Ettāvatāpi kho, āvuso, kāyasakkhī vutto bhagavatā pariyāyena.
root_text.an9.43:4.1: Puna caparaṁ, āvuso, bhikkhu sabbaso rūpasaññānaṁ samatikkamā paṭighasaññānaṁ atthaṅgamā nānattasaññānaṁ amanasikārā ‘ananto ākāso’ti ākāsānañcāyatanaṁ upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an9.43:4.2: Yathā yathā ca tadāyatanaṁ tathā tathā naṁ kāyena phusitvā viharati.
root_text.an9.43:4.3: Ettāvatāpi kho, āvuso, kāyasakkhī vutto bhagavatā pariyāyena …pe….
root_text.an9.43:5.1: Puna caparaṁ, āvuso, bhikkhu sabbaso nevasaññānāsaññāyatanaṁ samatikkamma saññāvedayitanirodhaṁ upasampajja viharati, paññāya cassa disvā āsavā parikkhīṇā honti.
root_text.an9.43:5.2: Yathā yathā ca tadāyatanaṁ tathā tathā naṁ kāyena phusitvā viharati.
root_text.an9.43:5.3: Ettāvatāpi kho, āvuso, kāyasakkhī vutto bhagavatā nippariyāyenā”ti.

translation_text.an9.43:1.2: What is the direct witness that the Buddha spoke of?”
translation_text.an9.43:2.1: “First, take a mendicant who, quite secluded from sensual pleasures … enters and remains in the first absorption.
translation_text.an9.43:2.2: They meditate directly experiencing that dimension in every way.
translation_text.an9.43:2.3: To this extent the Buddha spoke of the direct witness in a qualified sense.
translation_text.an9.43:3.1: Furthermore, take a mendicant who, as the placing of the mind and keeping it connected are stilled, enters and remains in the second absorption …
translation_text.an9.43:3.4: They meditate directly experiencing that dimension in every way.
translation_text.an9.43:3.5: To this extent the Buddha spoke of the direct witness in a qualified sense.
translation_text.an9.43:4.1: Furthermore, take a mendicant who, going totally beyond perceptions of form, with the disappearance of perceptions of impingement, not focusing on perceptions of diversity, aware that ‘space is infinite’, enters and remains in the dimension of infinite space.
translation_text.an9.43:4.2: They meditate directly experiencing that dimension in every way.
translation_text.an9.43:4.3: To this extent the Buddha spoke of the direct witness in a qualified sense. Furthermore, take a mendicant who enters and remains in the dimension of infinite consciousness … the dimension of nothingness … the dimension of neither perception nor non-perception …
translation_text.an9.43:5.1: Furthermore, take a mendicant who, going totally beyond the dimension of neither perception nor non-perception, enters and remains in the cessation of perception and feeling. And, having seen with wisdom, their defilements come to an end.
translation_text.an9.43:5.2: They meditate directly experiencing that dimension in every way.
translation_text.an9.43:5.3: To this extent the Buddha spoke of the direct witness in a definitive sense.”
